rent.note: furnished 1BR in El Poblado or Laureles; range $500–$1,200

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; $90–$180 range, spring weather keeps A/C moderate

diningOut.note: mix of local spots and nomad cafes; set lunches $3–6

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly $90–$180; Poblado has dense coworking scene

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: A mid-range lifestyle costs $1,200–$1,800 with popular safe areas, mix of cooking and eating out

evidence.claim: 1BR in El Poblado or Laureles $500–$1,200; suburban areas $300–$700
evidence.quote: For a typical one-bedroom, expect about $500–1,200/month in central neighborhoods like El Poblado or Laureles, while moving 15–30 minutes out (Envigado, Sabaneta, parts of Belén) can drop prices to $300–700/month
Utilities plus internet typically sit at $90–$180/month without A/C, with A/C adding $40–$120/month
Medellin's metro plus cable car system costs $35/month; Coworking is a predictable $90–$180/month for standard memberships
